name: Lac de l'Astarac — loop — running from Aussos
name_original: Lac de l'Astarac — boucle — course à pied depuis Aussos
region_intro: Jogging around Barrage-Réservoir De L'Astarac offers routes through a landscape defined by its distinctive Y-shaped artificial lake, which stretches across the Arrats and Petit Arrats river valleys. The area features wooded hillsides, particularly on the eastern side of the lake, providing varied terrain for runners. A dedicated 3 km fitness trail around the lake is available, offering an accessible path for running amidst natural surroundings. The region is also recognized for its ecological importance as a sensitive natural area. Best jogging routes around Barrage-Réservoir De L'Astarac The most popular jogging route is Lac de l'Astarac — loop — running from Aussos, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete, offering views of the lake. Another top favourite among local runners is Lac de l'Astarac loop from Aussos, a moderate 8.4 miles (13.6 km) path that circles the reservoir, providing extensive lake and valley scenery. Local runners also love the Small road in the woods loop from Bézues-Bajon, an 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail leading through wooded hillsides, often completed in about 1 hour 34 minutes. Jogging around Barrage-Réservoir De L'Astarac is defined by its Y-shaped lake, wooded hillsides, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to moderate running routes. The routes in Barrage-Réservoir De L'Astarac are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 85 runners have used komoot to explore Barrage-Réservoir De L'Astarac's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Barrage-Réservoir De L'Astarac?
A: There are over 20 running routes available around Barrage-Réservoir De L'Astarac, offering a variety of options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What is the typical difficulty of running trails in this area?
A: Most of the running routes around Barrage-Réservoir De L'Astarac are considered moderate in difficulty, with a few easier options available. This means you can expect some elevation changes and varied terrain, suitable for runners with a basic level of fitness.

Q: Are there any dedicated fitness trails for jogging?
A: Yes, a significant draw for joggers is the presence of a 3 km fitness trail, also known as a 'parcours de santé', located around the lake. This dedicated path provides an accessible and enjoyable route for running amidst natural surroundings.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect while jogging around Barrage-Réservoir De L'Astarac?
A: The landscape is defined by the distinctive Y-shaped artificial lake, stretching over the Arrats and Petit Arrats river valleys. You'll find wooded hillsides, particularly on the eastern side, and from the main dike, you can enjoy exceptional panoramic views of the Pyrénées mountains on clear days. The area is also a sensitive natural area, offering opportunities for birdwatching.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around the lake?
A: Yes, several routes offer circular paths around the lake. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1195428326/lac-de-l-astarac-loop-running-from-aussos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de l'Astarac — loop — running from Aussos&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 4.0-mile (6.4 km) option, and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20161295" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de l'Astarac loop from Aussos&lt;/a&gt; covers 8.4 miles (13.6 km), providing extensive lake and valley scenery.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about the routes here?
A: The running routes around Barrage-Réservoir De L'Astarac are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful lake views, and the peaceful natural environment that makes for an enjoyable outdoor experience.

Q: Can I find running routes that go through wooded areas?
A: Absolutely. The eastern side of the lake is bordered by a wooded hillside, and routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20161286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small road in the woods loop from Bézues-Bajon&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.9-mile (14.3 km) trail, lead you through these scenic wooded areas, offering shade and a different running experience.

Q: Are there any opportunities for wildlife spotting while running?
A: Yes, Barrage-Réservoir De L'Astarac is recognized as a sensitive natural area and part of the Natura 2000 network, making it a crucial migratory stopover and wintering ground for over 160 different bird species. You might spot booted eagles, red kites, grey herons, and egrets. The wooded hillsides are also home to various raptors and forest bats.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging around Barrage-Réservoir De L'Astarac?
A: The area is enjoyable for jogging throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer mornings and evenings are ideal to avoid the midday heat, while the dedicated fitness trail and wooded routes can provide good options even in cooler months.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for more experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those looking for a longer challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24570354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de Saint-Blancard loop from Lunax&lt;/a&gt; offer a moderate 11.4 km (7.1 miles) run, providing a more extensive exploration of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any shorter, easier running options for beginners or a quick run?
A: Yes, for a quicker or easier run, the 3 km fitness trail around the lake is an excellent option. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1195428326/lac-de-l-astarac-loop-running-from-aussos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de l'Astarac — loop — running from Aussos&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.0-mile (6.4 km) route that can be completed in about 45 minutes, making it suitable for a refreshing jog.
tips: A very beautiful course in a tunnel of woods, trees and greenery.

This artificial lake, whose dam was built in 1976, is a stopover for migratory birds. It is part of the Natura 200 network and a wide variety of migratory birds can be observed here, for example the booted eagle, the red kite, the gray heron and the egrets. A 3 km fitness trail is available.
